subject: Re: Greg Hersch/Renaissance
body:
fine

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> I reviewed the Renaissance fund Subscription Docs
> and Offering Docs  which 
> Greg sent to me on Friday.  I note that there are a
> few points of  which you may 
> not be aware:
>  
> 1.    There is a 1% placement fee, which Greg cannot
>  get waived.  That is, 
> $10,000 on the $1MM subscription
>  
> 2.    Greg Hersch's firm get's 60% of that Placement
>  Fee and Citigroup 
> Alternative Investments ("CAI") gets 40% of the
> placement  fee.
>  
> 3.    Hersch/CAI also get 1% per annum management
> fee  payable monthly.  The 
> split for that fee is:  Hersch's Firm - 40%/CAI  -
> 60%
>  
> 4.    In addition to that management fee, a 0.5% 
> management fee is paid to 
> the Portfolio Manager.
>  
> 5.    In addition to the portfolio manager's fixed
> fee,  the Portfolio 
> Manager also receives a 10% incentive allocation.
>  
> 6.    Withdrawals are permitted quarterly, but only 
> after a one-time 6-month 
> lock-up.  All withdrawals require notice of at 
> least "65 calendar days plus 
> 3 business days prior to quarter-end"
>  
> Greg did not inform us of the 6-month lock-up, the
> placement fee or the  
> remaining fee structure (that is, 1%. 0.5% and 10%)
> until after he sent me the  
> documents, but Greg did volunteer "in full and fair
> disclosure", without being  
> asked, that Greg's firm was going to get 60% of the
> placement fee and  40% of 
> the 1% management fee
>  
>  
> Darren K.  Indyke

body_html:
fine

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

I reviewed the Renaissance fund Subscription Docs
and Offering Docs which
Greg sent to me on Friday. I note that there are a
few points of which you may
not be aware:

  1. There is a 1% placement fee, which Greg cannot

get waived. That is,
$10,000 on the $1MM subscription

  1. Greg Hersch's firm get's 60% of that Placement

Fee and Citigroup
Alternative Investments ("CAI") gets 40% of the
placement fee.

  1. Hersch/CAI also get 1% per annum management

fee payable monthly. The
split for that fee is: Hersch's Firm - 40%/CAI -
60%

  1. In addition to that management fee, a 0.5%

management fee is paid to
the Portfolio Manager.

  1. In addition to the portfolio manager's fixed

fee, the Portfolio
Manager also receives a 10% incentive allocation.

  1. Withdrawals are permitted quarterly, but only

after a one-time 6-month
lock-up. All withdrawals require notice of at
least "65 calendar days plus
3 business days prior to quarter-end"

Greg did not inform us of the 6-month lock-up, the
placement fee or the
remaining fee structure (that is, 1%. 0.5% and 10%)
until after he sent me the
documents, but Greg did volunteer "in full and fair
disclosure", without being
asked, that Greg's firm was going to get 60% of the
placement fee and 40% of
the 1% management fee

Darren K. Indyke

electronic signature under applicable
